title: leontodon protheiformis subsp. hastilis
intro: Leontodon protheiformis subsp. hastilis, also known as the lesser hawkbit, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is a perennial herb with edible flowers and leaves that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Leontodon protheiformis subsp. hastilis can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits and potential interactions with other medications.
